title: ctenopterella boivinii
intro: Ctenopterella boivinii, commonly known as the Boivin's ctenopterella, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that is native to Madagascar. The Boivin's ctenopterella has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its therapeutic actions that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its health benefits and medicinal properties.

The leaves of the Ctenopterella boivinii plant are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin, and have been used to treat fever and inflammatory conditions. The roots contain alkaloids, such as berberine, which are used to treat infections and skin conditions. side_effects: Ctenopterella boivinii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in individuals with sensitive stomachs.
